monthly_review.summary: You've significantly increased intensity this month, trading volume for quality — pace improved sharply while total distance dropped, and HR zone distribution shifted toward higher intensities.
monthly_review.highlight: Cadence increased by 23 spm month-over-month — the largest absolute improvement across all metrics, reflecting a meaningful shift toward more economical turnover.

goal_tracker.message: You need 32.0 more km across 12 remaining sessions to match last month’s volume — averaging 2.7 km per session over the next 16 days.

insights.title: Efficiency improved despite higher intensity
insights.body: Aerobic efficiency rose from 12.54 to 13.0 m/sec per bpm even as average HR increased +9 bpm and avg pace improved — indicating better cardiovascular economy at faster efforts.

insights.title: Zone 3 dominance with underutilized Zone 1
insights.body: This month, only 1% of time was spent in Zone 1 (recovery), down from 8% last month, while Zone 3 rose to 33% (vs 36% last month) and Zone 4 jumped to 14% (vs 11%). This suggests reduced recovery stimulus and potential fatigue accumulation.

insights.title: Cadence surge aligns with shorter, quicker strides
insights.body: Cadence increased from 128 to 151 spm, while stride length averaged 73–74 cm in recent runs — down from 79.8 cm on 2026-03-04 — confirming a deliberate shift toward higher turnover and reduced overstriding.

insights.title: Aerobic TE remains moderate despite pace gains
insights.body: Despite faster paces and elevated HR, average aerobic TE is only 3.0 (this month) vs 2.7 (last month) — suggesting your current load is still sustainable, but the jump in Zone 4 time (14%) signals emerging threshold work that needs careful recovery pairing.

recommendations.action: Do one 45-min Zone 2 run (HR ≤135 bpm) at 9:00–9:45 min/km pace within the next 3 days
recommendations.reason: To restore Zone 1/2 volume — currently only 1% Z1 and 50% Z2 this month — and counterbalance recent high-intensity time (47% in Z3+Z4).

recommendations.action: Perform a 3-km progression run: 1 km @ 8:45 min/km (Z3), 1 km @ 8:15 min/km (upper Z3/lower Z4), 1 km @ 7:50 min/km (Z4), with full recovery between segments
recommendations.reason: Your best recent pace is 7:55 min/km (475 sec/km on 2026-02-28), and Z4 time increased to 14% — this session safely targets VO₂ max stimulus without exceeding current capacity.

recommendations.action: Add 2 × 30-sec barefoot strides on grass post-run (after any easy run this week), focusing on light footstrike and 160+ spm cadence
recommendations.reason: Recent ground contact time (314–325 ms) and vertical oscillation (7.7–8.3 cm) are stable but could improve — strides reinforce neuromuscular efficiency gains seen in cadence (+23 spm).

weekly_focus.description: Balance newly developed speed and turnover with intentional low-HR volume to solidify aerobic gains and prevent residual fatigue from increased Z3/Z4 exposure.
